narrative_design_spec.synopsis: A bawdy screwball sci-fi comedy set aboard the deep-space research station U.S.S. Calliope. Newly assigned executive officer Commander Tess Vale arrives expecting routine dysfunction and finds a full-spectrum disaster: an alien empathy artifact has fused with the station AI, turning every system announcement into innuendo and forcing the crew's secrets into the open. As vain Captain Rex Vance, chaotic xenobiologist Dr. Oona Quill, shameless engineer Buck Malarkey, deadpan security chief Petra Null, and severe morality inspector Selene Pryce collide in a station-wide farce, Tess must hold the Calliope together before flirtation, honesty, and bureaucracy trigger a catastrophic meltdown.

character_data.name: Commander Tess Vale
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: Human woman in her early 30s with sharp features, athletic build, dark hair in a practical undercut, navy executive officer uniform worn slightly rumpled with rolled sleeves, alert eyes, skeptical expression, understated confidence, practical utility belt.
dialogue_examples: I'm not saying this station is cursed, but the coffee machine just winked at me.
Let's try one plan that doesn't end with paperwork, plasma fire, or an apology bouquet.
If the alien rock wants honesty, it's about to get several years of professional disappointment.
Captain, with respect, charisma is not a safety protocol.

character_data.name: Captain Rex Vance
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: Human man in his late 30s, square-jawed and conspicuously handsome, coiffed blond hair, immaculate white captain's uniform tailored too perfectly, decorative medals, expensive grin, swaggering posture, theatrical self-assurance.
dialogue_examples: Leadership is ninety percent posture, ten percent timing, and a little tasteful chest.
In my defense, it sounded like a terrible idea when I approved it.
Panic is contagious, darling. Luckily, so am I.
The uniform says authority, but the hair says vision.

character_data.name: Dr. Oona Quill
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: Human woman in her late 20s with a wiry build, copper-brown skin, bright mischievous eyes, curly black hair with one shaved side, oversized lab coat over fitted stationwear, ink-stained gloves, restless manic scientist energy.
dialogue_examples: Science isn't about asking whether we should. It's about pretending that question came later.
Good news: the artifact is responsive. Bad news: it responds like a divorced poet on champagne.
I didn't break the station. I merely introduced it to a more interesting theory of communication.
If this starts laying eggs, I want credit on the paper.

character_data.name: Chief Engineer Buck Malarkey
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: Human man in his 40s, broad-shouldered and rugged, dark beard with silver streaks, oil-smudged skin, sleeveless engineering coveralls, tool harness across his chest, tattooed forearms, crooked grin, heavy working hands.
dialogue_examples: I've fixed engines, airlocks, and one regrettable marriage. This'll fold.
If it sparks, hisses, or moans, I can probably repair it.
Who wired a research station like a casino with a drinking problem?
I respect the laws of physics. I just like to negotiate.

character_data.name: Lieutenant Petra Null
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: Human woman in her early 30s, tall and lean, pale skin, straight black bob haircut, severe security uniform, precise posture, impassive gaze, subtle scar across one eyebrow, controlled and intimidating presence.
dialogue_examples: I prefer my emergencies armed, labeled, and emotionally repressed.
For the record, I hated this plan before it achieved new and creative levels of stupid.
If anyone runs, I'll assume guilt and poor cardio.
I don't blush. That's just blood remembering its duties.

character_data.name: Inspector Selene Pryce
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: Human woman in her late 30s, elegant and intimidating, deep brown skin, silver-streaked hair in a sleek twist, immaculate charcoal bureaucratic uniform, sharp cheekbones, controlled expression, pearl-like data pin at the collar, severe grace.
dialogue_examples: Standards exist because without them people become themselves in public.
The Calliope's incident report reads like a seduction letter to negligence.
I am not here to judge. I am here to document, cite, and ruin careers.
Professionalism is just desire in a pressed uniform.

location_data.name: Command Deck
location_data.location_image_prompt: Retro-futurist command deck aboard the U.S.S. Calliope, sweeping star-view viewport, gleaming consoles with teal and magenta interfaces, chrome trim, velvet accents, art-deco curves, an absurdly ornate captain's chair, and dense research-station instrumentation.

location_data.name: Xenobiology Lab
location_data.location_image_prompt: Chaotic deep-space xenobiology laboratory with specimen tanks, alien flora in containment cylinders, holographic microscopes, cluttered workbenches, medical scanners, biohazard lockers, and a crystalline artifact on a sealed pedestal.

location_data.name: Maintenance Spine
location_data.location_image_prompt: Industrial service corridor inside a space station with exposed conduits, reactor pipes, ladder wells, blinking maintenance panels, drifting steam, emergency hazard stripes, narrow crawlspace branches, and heavy blast doors.

location_data.name: Velvet Nebula Lounge
location_data.location_image_prompt: Tacky-chic space station lounge with a curved chrome bar, neon signage, plush booths, holographic jukebox, cocktail dispensers, low cabaret stage, and a panoramic observation window showing the stars.

scene_data.narrtive_summary: Commander Tess Vale reports for duty on the U.S.S. Calliope and is greeted by a bridge-wide emergency already sliding into absurdity as the station AI turns routine alerts into flirtatious double entendres. Captain Rex Vance briskly recruits Tess to help contain the situation, Dr. Oona Quill admits an alien artifact may be involved, and Buck confirms half the station is now 'emotionally miswired.' The intro establishes the station's tone, the crew's chaotic dynamic, and the urgent news that a strict Galactic Oversight inspector is due to dock within hours.

On the Command Deck, Tess tries to impose a sane briefing while Rex deflects, Oona overexplains, and Petra quietly inventories everything that could explode. The crew learns the artifact, nicknamed the Euphonic Core, amplifies subtext and compels unnerving honesty through the station's systems. Rex then reveals the incoming inspector is Selene Pryce, his formidable ex-lover and the least forgiving bureaucrat in the quadrant, raising both the comic stakes and the social danger.

In the Xenobiology Lab, Tess assists Oona and Buck in an attempt to isolate the Euphonic Core. Instead, the artifact syncs more deeply with the station AI, which begins airing mortifying snippets from private logs and personnel evaluations. Oona discovers brute-force shutdowns only intensify the field, while Buck identifies that a manual power reroute through the Maintenance Spine might break the feedback loop long enough to survive Selene's inspection.

Tess heads into the Maintenance Spine with Buck and Petra to perform the reroute by hand. The screwball obstacle course includes malfunctioning doors that now respond best to emotionally honest voice commands, forcing deadpan admissions and accidental vulnerability from the team. They complete part of the reset but uncover evidence that someone with docking-level clearance has been boosting the signal intentionally, suggesting the coming inspection is tied to the crisis.

To keep Selene occupied after she arrives, Rex improvises a deeply unconvincing 'morale gala' in the Velvet Nebula Lounge. Tess tries to manage the inspector, calm the crew, and keep the AI from narrating everyone's chemistry out loud, while Oona treats the event like a field study and Petra keeps watch for sabotage. Selene appears scandalized, but Tess notices she knows the station's blind spots a little too well and suspects she has her own agenda for the Euphonic Core.

Back on the Command Deck, Tess pieces together the truth: Selene has been covertly amplifying the signal so she can confiscate the Euphonic Core as a tool for fleet-wide message control, and Rex's history with her is exactly as compromising as everyone feared. Their old relationship erupts into rapid-fire accusations, mutual attraction, and professional hypocrisy. Tess is forced to choose between covering the scandal for institutional stability or exposing the truth and trusting the crew to survive the fallout.

In the Xenobiology Lab, Tess organizes a final screwball sting: Oona recalibrates the Euphonic Core, Buck rigs the intercom grid, Petra locks down the exits, and Rex lures Selene into claiming the artifact on record. The modified Core flips from innuendo amplifier to truth broadcaster, forcing everyone to admit what they actually want, professionally and personally. Selene's scheme collapses under its own hypocrisy, the station stabilizes, and Tess proves the Calliope can function best when nobody is allowed to hide behind polish.

With Selene departing in disgrace and the Euphonic Core safely contained, the crew regroups in the Velvet Nebula Lounge for a bruised but triumphant after-action drink. Rex is chastened but still preening, Oona is already writing a wildly unethical paper proposal, Buck has repaired three systems and broken two rules, and Petra almost smiles. Tess, now fully accepted as the station's indispensable fixer, chooses to stay aboard the Calliope just as the AI delivers one last suspiciously suggestive announcement, promising that order has returned only in the loosest possible sense.

beat_sheets.source_scene_summary: Commander Tess Vale reports for duty on the U.S.S. Calliope and is greeted by a bridge-wide emergency already sliding into absurdity as the station AI turns routine alerts into flirtatious double entendres. Captain Rex Vance briskly recruits Tess to help contain the situation, Dr. Oona Quill admits an alien artifact may be involved, and Buck confirms half the station is now 'emotionally miswired.' The intro establishes the station's tone, the crew's chaotic dynamic, and the urgent news that a strict Galactic Oversight inspector is due to dock within hours.
beat_sheets.dramatic_question: Can Tess get a coherent handle on the Calliope's ridiculous, innuendo-spewing emergency quickly enough to take command before an inspector arrives?
beat_sheets.scene_goal: Establish herself as the new XO, extract a usable explanation for the crisis, and force the crew toward an actionable response.
beat_sheets.scene_turn: What begins as Tess's first day orientation becomes an immediate command trial: the station is suffering an alien-linked systems crisis, and a high-stakes inspection is only hours away.

beats.purpose: Introduce Tess's POV, contrast her expectations with the Calliope's reality, and set up the impending derailment.
beats.summary: Tess steps onto the Command Deck expecting a formal first-day handoff and finds a bridge already tense with unresolved alarms. Rex greets her with polished confidence, but the crew's distracted body language says the station is already in trouble.
beats.revelation: The Calliope is in the middle of a live problem before Tess's introduction is even complete.
beats.player_goal: Get oriented and determine whether the emergency is real or just bridge theatrics.
beats.exit_state: Tess's first impression of the station is that even its routine operations are unstable and overdramatic.

beats.purpose: Deliver the tonal hook by showing the AI malfunction in action.
beats.summary: A station alert blares across the deck, but the AI renders it as a breathy, suggestive double entendre instead of a usable warning. The bridge crew react with practiced embarrassment while Tess has to parse the actual danger through the innuendo.
beats.revelation: The station AI is corrupting official alerts into flirtatious, humiliating language.
beats.player_goal: Figure out what the alert actually means and how severe the malfunction is.
beats.exit_state: Tess realizes the crisis is both operationally dangerous and socially disastrous.

beats.purpose: Introduce Rex's leadership style and let the player establish Tess's initial tone.
beats.summary: Rex pivots seamlessly from charm to crisis management, welcoming Tess and immediately treating her like the solution to a problem she just walked into. Tess chooses whether to answer his breezy confidence with strict professionalism, dry sarcasm, or public solidarity.
beats.revelation: Rex is relying on Tess immediately instead of giving her a clean, orderly arrival.
beats.player_goal: Claim footing as the new XO without being swallowed by Rex's style.
beats.choice_prompt: How does Tess respond to Rex's too-casual handoff in the middle of the emergency?
branch_outcomes: Tess answers with hard professionalism, putting Rex on notice that she expects structure.
Tess answers with dry wit, matching the station's tone and easing crew tension.
Tess backs Rex in public, buying goodwill while reserving judgment privately.
beats.exit_state: Tess publicly steps into the XO role and defines her first impression on the bridge.

beats.purpose: Introduce Oona and seed the central sci-fi mystery without fully explaining it yet.
beats.summary: Oona rushes in from Xenobiology and admits that a recently studied alien artifact may have interacted with station systems. Her explanation is equal parts apology, fascination, and overcomplicated science, confirming that the problem is weirder than a simple software fault.
beats.revelation: An alien artifact from the Xenobiology Lab may be causing or amplifying the AI's bizarre behavior.
beats.player_goal: Get Oona to give a usable cause instead of losing the room in jargon.
beats.exit_state: The emergency now has a possible source, but no one has a safe fix.

beats.purpose: Widen the scope from a bridge embarrassment to a station-wide systems crisis.
beats.summary: Buck adds the engineer's perspective: this is not confined to the bridge, and multiple subsystems are behaving as if they have moods, appetites, or emotional damage. His blunt diagnosis that half the station is 'emotionally miswired' gives the crew a vivid name for the spreading problem.
beats.revelation: The malfunction is station-wide and affecting infrastructure, not just announcements or interface language.
beats.player_goal: Understand how far the problem has spread and what systems can still be trusted.
beats.exit_state: The crew can no longer pretend this is a localized glitch; it is a full-station emergency.

beats.purpose: Demonstrate Tess's competence by having her impose order and choose an initial response posture.
beats.summary: Tess cuts through the overlapping excuses, theories, and one-liners to demand triage, accountability, and a sane flow of information. The player chooses whether Tess first emphasizes command discipline, scientific containment, or engineering stabilization, shaping how the crew reads her leadership.
beats.revelation: The Calliope's crew is used to improvising around chaos and immediately responds to firm structure.
beats.player_goal: Establish authority and set the first response priority.
beats.choice_prompt: What immediate priority does Tess impose on the crisis response?
branch_outcomes: Tess demands a formal status chain, signaling that improvisation ends now.
Tess orders Oona to focus first on containing the artifact's influence.
Tess leans on Buck to stabilize essential systems before anything else.
beats.exit_state: For the first time since Tess arrived, the bridge starts acting like a command staff instead of a panicked variety act.

beats.purpose: Add the ticking-clock external stake and launch the next scene's briefing.
beats.summary: Just as the crew begins to cohere, Rex reveals that a strict Galactic Oversight inspector is due to dock within hours. The room's energy shifts from embarrassment to dread, and Tess recognizes she needs a real briefing immediately if the station is going to survive first contact with bureaucracy.
beats.revelation: A high-stakes Galactic Oversight inspection is imminent, leaving almost no time to contain the disaster.
beats.player_goal: Push the crew toward a complete, actionable briefing before the inspector arrives.
beats.exit_state: Tess is no longer orienting to a new post; she is now racing to save the Calliope before the inspection clock runs out.

beat_sheets.source_scene_summary: On the Command Deck, Tess tries to impose a sane briefing while Rex deflects, Oona overexplains, and Petra quietly inventories everything that could explode. The crew learns the artifact, nicknamed the Euphonic Core, amplifies subtext and compels unnerving honesty through the station's systems. Rex then reveals the incoming inspector is Selene Pryce, his formidable ex-lover and the least forgiving bureaucrat in the quadrant, raising both the comic stakes and the social danger.
beat_sheets.dramatic_question: Can Tess force a coherent briefing out of the Calliope's chaos before the incoming inspector turns a bizarre systems failure into a career-ending scandal?
beat_sheets.scene_goal: Get a clear explanation of the Euphonic Core crisis, establish the immediate dangers, and set the crew on a plan before the inspection begins.
beat_sheets.scene_turn: What starts as a chaotic but manageable emergency briefing turns into a politically explosive personal crisis when Tess learns the incoming inspector is Rex's ex, Selene Pryce.

beats.purpose: Open on the scene's comic chaos and establish Tess actively trying to impose order.
beats.summary: A flirtatious station alert interrupts the Command Deck just as Tess tries to begin a formal briefing. Rex smooths, Oona babbles, and Petra silently tracks risk until Tess steps in and claims the floor.
beats.revelation: The command crew is already struggling to communicate cleanly because the station keeps turning ordinary status chatter into loaded innuendo.
beats.choice_prompt: How does Tess take control of the room?
branch_outcomes: Sharp command presence: the crew snaps into line, but Rex visibly chafes.
Dry humor: the tension loosens, though the room stays a little unruly.
Practical triage: Tess wins immediate buy-in by demanding useful facts first.
beats.exit_state: Tess has everyone's attention, and the scene's core dynamic is set: she wants order, while everyone else copes badly in their own style.

beats.purpose: Explain the science-fiction rules of the crisis in plain terms.
beats.summary: Under pressure from Tess, Oona stops free-associating long enough to define the problem: the alien artifact, now nicknamed the Euphonic Core, has entangled with the station AI. It is amplifying emotional subtext into literal system behavior and dialogue, making the Calliope blurt what people mean instead of what they intended to say.
beats.revelation: The Euphonic Core is fused enough with the AI to turn subtext into broadcast text and push unnerving honesty through station systems.
beats.player_goal: Get a usable explanation of what the artifact is doing.
beats.exit_state: Tess understands the problem is not random malfunction but a station-wide honesty amplifier linked to the AI.

beats.purpose: Ground the farce in practical danger and raise the operational stakes.
beats.summary: Petra gives a dry, unnervingly thorough list of what could go wrong if the effect spreads: access locks, environmental systems, command permissions, and anything explosive or mood-sensitive. Her report turns the room from embarrassed to alert, because the Core is no longer just humiliating people; it may soon compromise safety itself.
beats.revelation: If the feedback loop continues, safety systems and command-level controls may begin responding to emotional intent rather than proper protocol.
beats.exit_state: The crew now treats the Core as a real station threat, not just an obscene annoyance.

beats.purpose: Show the honesty effect hitting the crew personally and expose hidden dynamics.
beats.summary: Tess starts cross-examining the officers, but the station keeps undercutting everyone's mask: Oona's professional alarm is mixed with scientific delight, Rex is more worried about looking ridiculous than admitting fear, and Petra is already planning for casualties. The briefing proves that even the senior staff cannot fully hide from the Core's influence.
beats.revelation: The Core is not merely garbling announcements; it is surfacing suppressed truths in real time, especially around command decisions and interpersonal tension.
beats.player_goal: Figure out whether the crew can still function while the station keeps exposing what they really feel.
beats.exit_state: Everyone on the deck is now painfully aware that pretense is becoming unreliable.

beats.purpose: Introduce the external pressure point that sharpens the whole act: Selene Pryce.
beats.summary: When Tess asks who exactly is coming to inspect the station, Rex dodges until Petra's silence and the AI's suggestive needling leave him nowhere to hide. He finally admits the incoming inspector is Selene Pryce, his ex-lover and a notorious enforcer of standards who will take one look at the Calliope's current state as both professional failure and personal insult.
beats.revelation: The inspector is Selene Pryce, Rex's formidable ex and one of the least forgiving bureaucrats in the quadrant.
beats.player_goal: Identify the political and personal threat arriving at the station.
beats.exit_state: The crisis expands from technical chaos into a socially explosive inspection disaster centered on Rex.

beats.purpose: Let Tess reassert leadership after the reveal and launch the next scene's action.
beats.summary: Tess absorbs the revelation, cuts off the spiral of reactions, and turns the problem back into orders: the Euphonic Core must be contained before Selene docks, and Rex's history cannot be allowed to derail the response. She directs the effort toward a lab-side isolation attempt, setting up the next move toward the Xenobiology Lab.
beats.revelation: There is no separating the technical fix from the personal fallout; both must be managed on the same clock before Selene arrives.
beats.player_goal: Decide how Tess answers Rex's confession while keeping the crew moving.
beats.choice_prompt: How does Tess respond to Rex's admission about Selene?
branch_outcomes: Professional containment: Tess suppresses commentary and redirects everyone toward solving the Core problem.
Dry mockery: Tess punctures Rex's ego, briefly easing tension while deepening his embarrassment.
Hard accountability: Tess warns Rex that any further omissions will cost him operational trust.
beats.exit_state: Tess leaves the briefing with a clear understanding of the Core, a sharper deadline, and a concrete next step: meet Oona in the Xenobiology Lab and try to isolate the artifact before the inspector arrives.
